meaning: n.克力欧卡舞,(巴西的)卡里奥克舞
examples: I've visited Brazil many times, and on my last trip I convinced myself that I was an honorary carioca.
I've visited Brazil many times, and on my last trip I convinced myself that I was an honorary Carioca, a true Rio native.
Dine in the hotel's new gourmet restaurant, which serves an inventive menu of Carioca cuisine and notable wine list with more than 130 labels.
Rio de Janeiro is designing a new programme, called Bolsa Carioca, to do exactly that.
The traditional Carioca (name of Rio locals) hotel already exists in the central part of Rio de Janeiro city.
